subject: Science
chapter: Nature of Matter: Elements, Compounds, and Mixtures

activities_projects.title: Lime Water Test for Carbon Dioxide in Air
activities_projects.objective: To confirm the presence of carbon dioxide in air
steps: Fill a glass tumbler halfway with water
Add a small amount of calcium oxide slowly
Stir to form calcium hydroxide (lime water)
Filter the solution and place it in a petri dish
Leave it exposed to air for some time and observe changes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn that carbon dioxide in air reacts with lime water turning it milky
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation and explanation of the reaction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Observation of Dust Particles in Air
activities_projects.objective: To observe suspended dust particles present in air
steps: Take a clean black sheet of paper
Place it near an open window or in the garden
Leave it undisturbed for a few hours
Observe the particles settled on the sheet using a magnifying glass
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that air contains suspended particles and pollutants

activities_projects.title: Electrolysis of Water
activities_projects.objective: To show that water is made of hydrogen and oxygen
steps: Fill a beaker with water and add a few drops of dilute sulfuric acid
Fill two test tubes with the same water
Place the battery in the beaker
Invert the test tubes over the battery terminals
Observe gas bubbles forming and collecting in the test tubes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn that water decomposes into hydrogen and oxygen
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of gases and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Heating Sugar Experiment
activities_projects.objective: To understand that sugar is a compound made of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen
steps: Put a teaspoon of sugar in a boiling tube
Heat it gently using a spirit lamp
Observe the color change from white to brown and then black
Notice water droplets inside the tube
Observe the black carbon residue left behind
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand that sugar decomposes into carbon and water
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation and explanation of decomposition (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mixture of Iron and Sulfur
activities_projects.objective: To observe properties of a mixture
steps: Mix iron filings and sulfur powder on a watch glass
Observe the color and texture
Bring a magnet close to the mixture
Observe that iron particles are attracted by the magnet
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn that components of a mixture retain their individual properties
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct explanation of mixture properties (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Formation of Iron Sulfide Compound
activities_projects.objective: To show that heating a mixture of iron and sulfur forms a compound
steps: Take a mixture of iron filings and sulfur powder
Heat the mixture gently in a china dish
Allow it to cool
Grind the black mass formed
Observe its properties
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the difference between mixtures and compounds
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparison of properties of mixture and compound (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Magnet Test for Mixtures and Compounds
activities_projects.objective: To differentiate between iron-sulfur mixture and iron sulfide compound
steps: Bring a magnet close to Sample A
Observe that iron particles are attracted
Bring the magnet close to Sample B
Observe that the compound is not attracted
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn that compounds have different properties than their constituent elements

activities_projects.title: Reaction of Iron with Hydrochloric Acid
steps: Take iron filings in a test tube
Add dilute hydrochloric acid
Observe bubbles forming
Bring a burning splinter near the mouth of the test tube
Listen for the 'pop' sound
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify hydrogen gas formation during the reaction
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of hydrogen gas (10 marks)
